Title: Bank Rakyat hit with RM1mil fine over cybersecurity breach
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has imposed an administrative penalty of RM1 million on Bank Kerjasama Rakyat Malaysia Bhd (Bank Rakyat) for cybersecurity and customer information breaches occurred on Jan 20.
The central bank said Bank Rakyat had failed to implement robust cybersecurity standards as required under the risk management in technology policy document (RMiT PD).
Bank Rakyat also failed to safeguard customer information through adequate controls as required under the management of customer information and permitted disclosures policy document (MCIPD PD).
"Bank Negara discovered that Bank Rakyat had breached several requirements under the RMiT PD and MCIPD PD following a cybersecurity incident in which an external threat actor gained unauthorised access to its IT infrastructure.
"These breaches were attributed to inadequate cybersecurity controls and incident response," it said.
Bank Negara noted that Bank Rakyat has taken remedial measures to strengthen its cybersecurity and information and communication technology controls, resources and governance arrangements.
The central bank added that relevant factors were considered in deciding the penalty to be imposed.
These include the severity of the breaches and Bank Rakyat's lack of reasonable care in ensuring compliance with the RMiT PD and MCIPD PD requirements; current controls to ensure compliance with the requirements; past compliance record; and post-misconduct behaviour and the effectiveness of remedial actions to prevent the recurrence of non-compliances.
Bank Rakyat paid RM1 million for the penalty on Jan 26.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Experts: Better SPM results do not prove exam reforms worked
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The improved performance of the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) cohort should be interpreted with nuance and not reduced to simplistic conclusions, experts said.
The cohort — the first to sit for the examination without having gone through UPSR and PT3 —
recorded better results
compared with previous years, with the national average grade (GPK) improving to 4.42 from 4.49 in 2024.
Educationist and Skolar Malaysia co-founder Dr Tazli Azizan said the improvement should be viewed in context, adding that student outcomes cannot be attributed to a single factor such as the omission of UPSR and PT3.
"The fact that this cohort did not sit for UPSR and PT3 does not automatically mean they were less prepared, nor does this result alone prove that any one assessment approach is fully responsible for their performance.
"Student outcomes are influenced by many factors, including teaching quality, school support, parental involvement, intervention strategies and the broader learning environment," he told the New Straits Times today.
He said the results challenge the need for early high-stakes exams, but do not prove any single approach drove the improvement.
Tazli said school-based assessment and continuous support can play a role in student performance when implemented meaningfully and consistently.
However, he added there should not be an overreliance on traditional examinations, which often prioritise memorisation and exam techniques over deeper learning.
"If we are serious about education reform, our assessment system must value critical thinking, problem solving, communication, collaboration and adaptability, rather than just performance in an exam hall," he said.
Meanwhile, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia's (UKM) Institute for Inclusive Development and Advancement of Malaysia deputy director Dr Anuar Ahmad said it would be misleading to conclude that examinations such as UPSR and PT3 are no longer necessary based on the improved results.
He said different examinations serve distinct purposes, adding that UPSR was intended to ensure no student is left behind in basic literacy and numeracy.
"Without a standardised national assessment at the primary level, there is a risk that students with weak foundational skills may go undetected," he said.
Anuar added this could particularly affect students from disadvantaged backgrounds or under-resourced schools, where early intervention is most needed.
He said the lack of early-stage assessments may have led to weaker performance in Bahasa Melayu, Science and Mathematics.
"There is concern that some students may progress through the system without mastering basic skills, and this may only become evident at the SPM level," he said.
On school-based assessment, Anuar said there is currently no comprehensive data to establish a direct link between such assessments and improved SPM performance.
"We do not yet have sufficient evidence to confirm whether school-based assessment is the key factor behind this year's results," he said.
He said an impact study is needed, while pointing to implementation challenges such as large class sizes and a heavy syllabus.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Account service provider held by MACC for false claims
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded an account service provider from a business management consultancy firm for allegedly submitting documents containing false claims amounting to about RM4 million.
A four-day remand order, until April 4, 2026, was issued by magistrate Ezrene Zakariah following an application by MACC at the Putrajaya magistrate's court this morning.
According to a source, the male suspect, believed to be in his 30s, was arrested at about 8pm yesterday when he turned up to give his statement at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya.
"Initial investigations found that the suspect is believed to have committed the offences between 2023 and 2025.
"He is suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars involving various claims, including travel and transportation; accounting fees; purchase of generators and diesel; credit card payments; entertainment claims; expenses related to property, plant and equipment (PPE); petty cash claims; unidentified expenditure; payments for landscaping works; and salary payments," the source said.
Meanwhile, MACC deputy senior director (Operations Control and Coordination) Datuk Yuhafiz Mohd Salleh, when contacted, confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Raided: Store making customers buy other groceries before getting subsidised cooking oil
Article:
TAPAH: A retail premises here has come under scrutiny after enforcement officers uncovered a tactic forcing customers to purchase additional groceries before being allowed to buy subsidised cooking oil.
The operation, conducted yesterday at 10.30am by the Perak Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers, followed a public complaint alleging "conditional sales" involving 1kg packet cooking oil.
State ministry director, Datuk Kamalludin Ismail, said enforcement officers deployed undercover methods, including a test purchase, to verify the claim.
"The investigation confirmed that the premises had imposed conditions requiring customers to buy other items before they could obtain the subsidised cooking oil. Such practices are not permitted," he said in a statement.
Kamalludin said a total of 714kg of 1kg packet cooking oil, along with business documents, licences and purchase invoices, were seized during the operation.
"The total value of the seizure is estimated at RM1,785. The case is being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961," he said.
He added that the offence involves imposing conditions on the sale of controlled goods without written approval from the Supply Controller.
"If convicted, individuals may face a fine of up to RM1 million, imprisonment of up to three years, or both. Corporations, meanwhile, can be fined up to RM2 million, or up to RM5 million for repeat offences," he said.
Kamalludin urged the public to continue assisting authorities by reporting any suspicious activities involving controlled and subsidised goods.
"We welcome information from the public and encourage them to act as the eyes and ears of enforcement agencies to curb leakages and ensure essential goods reach those who need them," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM pays tribute to RTD on 80th anniversary, urges to enhance service quality
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid tribute to the Road Transport Department (RTD) on its 80th anniversary, and called on the agency to further strengthen enforcement and enhance its service quality to maintain public confidence in the agency.
In a Facebook post, Anwar said increasingly complex road conditions demand continued firmness, accountability and efficiency from the department.
"Happy 80th anniversary to RTD. Over eight decades, the department has indeed played a vital role in ensuring the nation's transport system operates in an orderly and safe manner, in line with public needs and evolving demands," he said.
Anwar also expressed appreciation for the commitment of RTD personnel, who continue to serve with dedication and integrity.
"May this institution remain steadfast in carrying out its responsibilities for the safety and well-being of the nation," he added. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms in multiple states until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across several states until 9pm.
The alert covers, among others, Kedah (Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu); Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah, and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak.
Other affected states are Kelantan (Gua Musang); Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, and Bera); Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat, and Sepang);  Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai, and Johor Baru); Sarawak (including Kuching, Bau, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong, Sarikei, Sibu, Kapit, Bintulu, Miri, and Limbang); and Sabah (Pedalaman, Tawau, and Sandakan).
Also affected are the federal territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
METMalaysia urged the public to exercise caution, particularly when outdoors, and to avoid areas prone to flooding or landslides.
"The public is advised to monitor weather updates closely and take necessary precautions to ensure safety during periods of heavy rainfall and thunderstorms," the department said in a statement posted on Facebook.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Explosions rock Teheran as Trump signals rapid end to US-Israel war with Iran
Article:
TEHERAN: Teheran was rocked by a fresh wave of explosions Wednesday, as US President Donald Trump said the war with Iran could be over in two or three weeks.
The conflict began when the United States and Israel launched airstrikes across Iran that killed supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, quickly mushrooming into a regional war that has sent oil and gas prices soaring and threatened to torpedo the global economy.
But Trump, whose statements on the war have swung from combative to conciliatory, said late Tuesday that the fighting could be over in "two weeks, maybe three."
The White House said he would give "an important update on Iran" to the nation at 9pm Wednesday (0100 GMT Thursday).
Teheran has insisted there are no ongoing negotiations with the United States, and that it has not responded to a reported 15-point proposal from Washington to end the war.
With the status of diplomatic efforts uncertain, Iranian state television reported that Teheran was under attack on Wednesday, and explosions were heard in the capital's north, east and centre.
The Israeli military confirmed it had carried out the strikes, and later said it was intercepting a missile attack from Iran which medics said seriously injured an 11-year-old girl and wounded 13 others.
Israel said Wednesday its air defences were also responding to a missile fired from Yemen – the third attack by the Iran-backed Houthi rebels since they entered the war over the weekend.
Thousands of people have been killed throughout the region during the conflict, which has displaced millions more from their homes.
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said the Islamic republic had the "necessary will" to end the war, provided its enemies guaranteed it would not flare up again.
But the Israeli prime minister insisted Tuesday that the military campaign would continue.
"We will continue to crush the terror regime," Benjamin Netanyahu said on the eve of the Jewish Passover holidays, adding that it had "changed the face of the Middle East."
- 'NOBODY KNOWS WHAT'S HAPPENING' -
In Lebanon, seven people were killed in strikes in south Beirut and a nearby area, the health ministry said Wednesday, while the Israeli military said it had struck a senior Hizbollah commander.
AFP correspondents at the site saw a blackened, debris-strewn street.
"Nobody knows what's happening," resident Hassan Jalwan told AFP, adding that "displaced people have been sleeping in the open" in the area.
Lebanon was drawn into the war on March 2 when Teheran-backed group Hizbollah launched attacks on Israel.
Israel has responded with broad strikes and a ground offensive that the Lebanese health ministry says has left more than 1,200 dead.
US allies in the Gulf have also been pulled into the war, with Iran carrying out retaliatory attacks on nations it says have been launchpads for strikes.
A Bangladeshi national was killed Wednesday by falling shrapnel from an intercepted drone in the United Arab Emirates.
Kuwait's civil aviation authority said the Gulf state's international airport had come under Iranian drone attack that led to "a large fire" at its fuel tanks.
Elsewhere, Bahrain's interior ministry said a fire broke out at a business facility "as a result of the Iranian aggression", and Saudi Arabia said several drones were "intercepted and destroyed."
A tanker was also hit in the waters off Qatar, a British maritime security agency said, reporting some damage but no casualties.
"Every day, we hear the sound of drones," Waad Abdulrazaq, a 31-year-old truck driver, told AFP near Iraq's Erbil international airport, which has faced regular attacks by pro-Iran groups.
"We hear them in the morning, and we hear them at night. We can no longer sleep or live in peace."
- 'HORRIBLE' -
Trump threatened earlier this week that if Iran didn't agree to a deal, US forces would "obliterate" its oil wells, its main Kharg Island export terminal, and possibly water desalination plants.
The United States has not said who it is speaking with in Iran, which has denied it is in talks.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera that he still receives messages from US envoy Steve Witkoff, "directly, as before, and this does not mean that we are in negotiations."
"Whenever necessary we respond to these messages," Araghchi said in the interview aired on Wednesday.
Oil prices tumbled after Trump's comments about ending the war, and stocks rallied in Europe and Asia.
But the US president also said that France, China and other countries that seek passage through the Strait of Hormuz will have to "fend for themselves", lashing out at allies that have refused to help Washington's war effort.
Iran has maintained a chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of global oil normally passes.
Rising fuel prices stoked by the standoff have become a headache for Trump.
At a gas station in Washington's suburbs, Jeanne Williams, 83, was aghast at the higher prices.
"That is horrible," she said.
But Trump said he is not worried.
"All I have to do is leave Iran," he told reporters.
"And we'll be doing that very soon, and they'll come tumbling down."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang revises quit rent mechanism to ease landowner burden
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has introduced improvements to its quit rent rates mechanism following concerns since its implementation earlier this year.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state executive council had reviewed the issue and agreed to adopt a reclassification approach based on building use.
This applies to First Grade land, Conditions A, B, C, as well as Nil Category (NIL) land without planning permission.
The revision mainly involves land used for industrial and commercial purposes that did not undergo proper planning approval.
He said under the new approach, quit rent rates would be aligned with the Building (Others) category, set at RM0.70 per square metre for urban land and RM0.50 for rural land, significantly lower than the previously proposed RM3.25.
"However, affected landowners will no longer qualify for the existing 50 per cent rebate, as they are already benefiting from the reduced rates," he told reporters at his office in Komtar here.
There had been numerous complaints from affected landowners that they had to pay very high quit rent rates. Also present was Penang Land and Mines Department director Datuk Dr Faizal Kamarudin.
Chow said the move reflected the state government's commitment to easing the financial burden on landowners, particularly those impacted by the revised rates.
He urged landowners to regularise and develop their land through proper legal channels to support sustainable state development.
As of March 31, a total of 4,001 feedback submissions and appeals had been received by district offices across the state.
Of these, 2,885 appeals were related to sharp increases in quit rent, including cases involving First Grade land, conversion of rural to urban status, durian agricultural land, hillside land, and requests for rate reviews.
Another 1,116 appeals involved technical issues, with the highest number — 574 cases — linked to buildings spanning multiple lots, followed by financial constraints, abandoned projects, non-profit organisations, and idle land.
"Some 3,043 cases are currently being processed by the District and Land Offices and the Penang Land and Mines Office," he added.
Chow said 163,262 quit rent account holders — representing 54 per cent of active taxpayers in 2025 — had settled their payments as of March 31.
"This is slightly lower than the same period last year, possibly as landowners await further announcements before making payments," he said.
He urged landowners to submit appeals before the April 30 deadline, although late submissions may still be considered.
Commenting on a March 30 meeting with DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, Chow said all parties accepted the explanation on the revised quit rent rates.
He added that while the meeting proceeded smoothly and provided a comprehensive briefing, no decisions were made, and all measures announced were determined by the state executive council after considering proposals to ease the burden on the public.
Last month, Loke ordered former chief minister Lim Guan Eng and Chow to end their ongoing public
spat
over Penang's controversial quit rent hikes.
Loke said party discipline must be upheld at all times and he would convene a meeting involving both leaders to resolve the matter amicably.
This comes amid a public spat between Lim and Chow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Segamat Hospital emergency dept stretched thin with 13 doctors on round-the-clock duty
Article:
SEGAMAT: Segamat Hospital's emergency and trauma department is facing a critical manpower crunch, with just 13 medical officers (MOs) covering 24-hour emergency services for the district.
A medical officer, speaking anonymously to CodeBlue due to civil service restrictions, said MOs routinely work 12- to 14-hour shifts, often alone, as part of a three-shift system.
CodeBlue is an independent Malaysian health policy and public health journalism platform.
"In some cases, the department must implement double shifts at least once a week to cope with patient volume," the officer said.
"The doctor-to-patient ratios are alarming. In the green zone, one MO handles about 25 patients; in yellow, one MO manages 15; and in red, there's one MO for five critical cases.
"The workload is unsustainable and could compromise patient safety," the officer added.
The zones indicate the severity of patient cases: green for low-risk or stable patients, yellow for moderate-risk or higher-care patients, and red for critical cases requiring intensive attention.
The shortage stems from an uneven distribution of MOs across the Segamat district, the officer claimed.
While Segamat and Labis health clinics have 20–30 MOs each, most hospital departments average only eight to 10 MOs, who also cover multiple on-call duties each month.
Emergency medicine specialists assist where possible, but they are burdened with administrative, training, and critical care responsibilities, leaving gaps in frontline coverage.
"The imbalance between hospital and clinic staffing must be addressed urgently," the officer said, calling on the Johor state health department to reassess allocations based on operational needs and patient volume.
With emergency services under sustained pressure, the officer warned that continued understaffing could lead to longer patient wait times, compromised care, and operational strain.
In response, the Johor State Health Department acknowledged the imbalance of MO distribution between health clinics and Segamat Hospital.
It said the fill rate for MO positions at Segamat health office clinics is 90.4 per cent, while at Segamat Hospital it stands at only 1 per cent, reflecting differences in workforce allocation between primary care and hospital services.
The department emphasised that duties at health clinics differ significantly from those in hospital clinical departments.
Daily patient attendance at Segamat and Labis clinics averages 400–500, and MOs at these clinics also handle extended hours, community clinics, mobile health teams, and public health programmes.
Recognising the urgent need for additional MOs at Segamat Hospital, the department said current emergency department staffing ratios are highly stretched.
"We are committed to implementing improvements to ensure patient care remains optimal," the statement read.
It added that allocations of contract MOs across Johor hospitals are based on vacancies and departmental workload, and that measures are being taken to integrate hospital and clinic human resources effectively.
Further discussions are planned to mobilise staff across departments and alleviate pressure on overstretched units, particularly in emergency care.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Driver dies, 2 injured in Sungai Besar car accident
Article:
KUALA SELANGOR: A car driver was killed and two others injured after the vehicle they were travelling in skidded, crashed into a road divider, and overturned at Kampung Belia 2, Sungai Panjang, Sungai Besar, today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received a call about the incident at 1.25pm.
"A team of seven personnel, along with a fire engine from the Sungai Besar Fire and Rescue Station, was dispatched to the scene.
"The accident involved a Proton Saga carrying a man, a woman, and a young girl.
"The driver, a local man in his 30s, was trapped and was confirmed dead by Health Ministry personnel," he said in a statement.
Mukhlis said two of the victims had been successfully extricated by members of the public before firefighters arrived.
He added that the trapped driver was later extricated by firefighters.
"The victim was successfully removed at 3.03pm and handed over to the police for further action.
"Meanwhile, the woman, also in her 30s, sustained serious injuries, while the young girl suffered minor injuries. They were handed over to Health Ministry personnel," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New communications command centre set up to coordinate economic measures
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Putrajaya has rolled out several measures to keep Malaysians informed about how it is addressing the economic fallout from the Middle East conflict, including the global energy crisis.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cabinet had agreed to set up a Communications Command Centre under the National Economic Action Council.
The centre, chaired by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, will coordinate the government's messaging on economic responses and measures in light of the conflict.
In addition, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohamad Nasir will meet the Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Finance and Economy to present the government's strategies and initiatives to date, particularly regarding economic and financial challenges arising from the conflict.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Aman examines phone linked to loan shark syndicate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A mobile phone allegedly belonging to a member of a loan shark syndicate has been handed over to Bukit Aman's forensic unit for analysis.
Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said authorities are currently awaiting the outcome of the analysis from the relevant department.
"The phone seized earlier has been sent to the forensic unit, and we are now waiting for the report regarding investigations into the three suspects who were arrested.
"If the findings are positive, the investigating officer will refer the investigation paper to the deputy public prosecutor for a recommendation on charges," he said when contacted.
The Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) had previously urged police to examine the contents of the mobile phone, which was allegedly linked to the syndicate, after discovering various materials related to the group's operations on the
device
.
Its secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the organisation had lodged a police report regarding the device, which is believed to contain incriminating evidence of the syndicate's activities.
The syndicate reportedly valued the phone and allegedly offered money to MHO activists to retrieve it.
"They contacted us and offered money for the phone to be returned, starting at around RM15,000, and the offer escalated to RM2 million," he told a press conference last month.
Hishamuddin said the device contained information and documents, including a 69-page operations manual detailing the syndicate's modus operandi.
#KW: Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook, mobile phone, Bukit Aman, loan shark, Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation, MHO, secretary-general, Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother informs teacher about child's encounter with Zara Qairina
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A mother informed a school teacher about her child's encounter with the late Zara Qairina Mahathir to seek clarification on the inquest.
The 66th witness told the Coroner's Court that she only learned between September and October last year that her eldest child could have been the last person to interact with Zara on July 16.
During the examination, lawyer Nurul Rafeeqa Afdul Mutolip asked the 41-year-old mother why she chose to inform the school earlier this year instead of doing so immediately after learning about the incident from her daughter.
"When my daughter told me about her interaction with Zara, she also mentioned that a female police officer had previously contacted her and made her cry.
"We thought everything had already been handed over to the police and the teachers.
"We were concerned. We did not know the progress of the inquest or the list (of witnesses), and we wondered whether our child would be called.
"That was why I contacted Ustazah Syukriah regarding the inquest. She was also surprised and informed the police," she testified before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 62nd day of the inquest in the afternoon.
Nurul Rafeeqa is representing Zara's father.
Earlier in the morning, she testified that there had been significant behavioural and mood changes in her
daughter
after learning that Zara had been found unconscious on the ground floor of the SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha school dormitory at about 3am on July 16.
Her
child
, who is also the 65th witness in the inquest, said she saw Zara moving back and forth on the third floor of the dormitory near the toilet, and later calmed and escorted her back to her room between 1am and 2am on the same day.
Meanwhile, when Nurul Rafeeqa asked why she did not refer her traumatised child to a specialist, the mother of four said they initially chose to manage and stabilise the 15-year-old's emotions on their own.
The inquest will resume tomorrow, with consultant forensic psychiatrist Dr Chua Sze Huang expected to testify.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: United Malacca set to benefit from higher CPO prices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: United Malacca Bhd is set to benefit from rising crude palm oil (CPO) prices, supported by strong growth in fresh fruit bunch (FFB) output, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd.
The firm said the palm oil producer achieved an 18.4 per cent year-on-year (YoY) increase in FFB output in the nine-month period of financial year 2026 (9M FY26), reflecting successful yield-improvement efforts at the estate level.
The company's 9MFY26 core net profit rose 51.2 per cent YoY to RM128.6 million, surpassing CIMB Securities' forecast at 126 per cent and reaching 97 per cent of the full-year consensus estimate.
CIMB Securities said United Malacca's Malaysian operations remained the backbone of United Malacca's plantation earnings, contributing 80 per cent of total earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA) in 9MFY26.
EBITDA from Malaysian plantations rose 31.1 per cent YoY to RM172.7 million, supported by a 19.6 per cent YoY increase in FFB production and a 16.2 per cent YoY rise in palm kernel prices to RM3,326 per tonne.
Higher production volumes also helped reduce unit costs, enhancing overall profitability.
Meanwhile, Indonesian operations saw EBITDA climb 27.3 per cent YoY to RM46.2 million, thanks to improved FFB output, higher PK prices, and stronger mill performance.
"We expect CPO prices to strengthen in 2026, supported by higher biodiesel mandates in Indonesia and the US, which will boost edible oil demand.
"In addition, potential El Niño conditions in the second half of 2026 could tighten supply," the firm said.
CIMB Securities raised United Malacca's earnings forecasts by 48 per cent for FY26 and 70 per cent for FY27, based on higher average CPO price assumptions of RM4,186 a tonne for 2026 and RM4,433 a tonne for 2027, as well as increased FFB output estimates of 521,906 tonnes for 2026 and 529,499 tonnes for 2027, factoring in higher fertiliser and diesel costs.
It kept a "Buy" call on the company with a higher target price of RM6.83.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'We still live in fear': RCI push renewed a year after gas explosion
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Environmental watchdogs and residents of Putra Heights — the neighbourhood devastated by a massive gas explosion from an underground pipeline on April 1 last year — have renewed calls for a Royal Commission of Inquiry.
The groups and residents said the call was not meant to assign blame, but to uncover the truth and raise awareness of potential future risks involving the 2,600km Peninsular Gas Utilisation (PGU) pipeline network.
Rimbawatch director Adam Farhan said calls for an RCI had been made since the first day of the explosion and remained unanswered a year on.
"We are not the first organisation to call for this. Calls for an RCI have been made since the first day of the explosion, and we are here, one year on, to repeat that demand." he told reporters after the launch of a documentary on the incident by Rimbawatch, Greenpeace and the Activist Network.
The event, held at Masjid Putra Heights, also saw the launch of a policy brief titled "Putra Heights: One Year On, Unanswered Questions Remain", outlining key unresolved issues surrounding the explosion.
Also present was Kota Kemuning assemblyman S. Preakas.
Adam claimed existing investigations had been limited, alleging that a parliamentary special select committee found no negligence, while police classified the case as requiring no further action.
He stressed an RCI would allow key agencies to present evidence and be cross-examined to establish a clearer account of events.
Among the key concerns raised were pipeline zoning decisions, with documents suggesting the infrastructure had been routed through areas already earmarked for residential development.
The groups also raised concerns over the monitoring of soil subsidence and the adequacy of buffer zones, as well as whether industry standards were properly applied and environmental impact assessments adequately conducted.
"We need to see evidence of compliance, review zoning and environmental documents, and understand what led up to the explosion," he said.
The groups also pointed to overlapping responsibilities among regulators and operators — including the Energy Commission and Petronas Gas Bhd under the Gas Supply Act 1993 and related laws — raising concerns over whether accountability had been fully examined.
[[nid:1408101]]
Meanwhile, Abdul Ghani Abdul Aziz, 58, who lost his home in the incident, said an RCI was crucial as residents continued to live with lingering fear and uncertainty.
The businessman said many were still grappling with the psychological impact of the blast, describing a constant sense of anxiety over the possibility of another incident.
"Every second we feel anxious when we are here, waiting for when an explosion might happen again. This is why it is so important to resolve this issue," he said, adding that unlike in war zones where warning systems exist, residents had no form of early alert.
Ghani, whose house was about 60m from the explosion site, said residents were not seeking to assign blame, but wanted clarity, proper safety measures and assurances moving forward.
Similarly, Ooi Sui Li, 43, said transparency and accountability were critical to prevent a recurrence.
The software engineer said residents could not have peace of mind without knowing the full truth behind the incident.
"We are not sure of the reports by authorities that have been presented. I don't think that is the complete truth. We hope that with this request, more truth can be revealed for the betterment of everybody — not just for the residents of Putra Heights, but for the whole nation," she said.
Ooi said the issue extended beyond a single neighbourhood, noting many Malaysians live near pipeline infrastructure and deserve to know what safeguards are in place.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Durian seller remanded for peeping on underage cousin while she bathed
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A durian seller has been remanded for three days by the Magistrate's Court here today for allegedly peeping on his female cousin while she was bathing at a house in Parit Sulong yesterday.
The remand order against the 37-year-old suspect, a father of one, was issued by Magistrate Nurasidah A Rahman, effective from today until Friday.
The remand is to facilitate investigations under Section 14 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
According to the report, the 17-year-old victim was bathing when her mother heard her scream from inside the bathroom.
The victim later told her 39-year-old mother that she saw a man peeping before the suspect fled the scene.
The suspect reportedly did not manage to record any footage.
However, the victim suspected her cousin after noticing his car parked near her house.
Police later arrested the suspect at a house to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC probe into Azam Baki's shares, 'corporate mafia' will be made public – Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: KUALA LUMPUR: The investigation into Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki's shareholdings and allegations of a so-called "corporate mafia" within the commission will be made public.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil assured that disclosure would take place at the appropriate time.
"It will be, when the time comes," he said.
Fahmi said that Putrajaya must first await input from the Chief Secretary to the Government and allow the proper procedures to be completed, noting that no findings from the investigation have yet been submitted to the cabinet.
"We must allow the Chief Secretary to the Government to do so and let the process take place.
"Therefore, we leave that to him.
"However, the current investigation is also linked to earlier probes concerning allegations of corporate manipulation and whether there are any connections.
"At this stage, no findings from the investigation have been presented to the cabinet.
"So, no decision can be made at this time," he said at the weekly post-cabinet meeting press conference.
Fahmi said that such a decision will be up to the prime minister and the cabinet to decide.
"We must ensure that the investigation is fully completed before any further action is taken, including disclosing the findings of the probe into the shareholdings of the chief commissioner.
"However, it is up to the cabinet to decide whether the investigative process is sufficient at any given time, and any such determination rests with the relevant ministers and the cabinet itself," he said.
Yesterday, Shamsul Azri said that proceedings have been initiated under the Public Officers (Conduct and Discipline) Regulations 1993 after the investigation committee presented findings during the cabinet meeting on March 11, 2026.
This follows a Bloomberg report that claims Anwar
directed the special
committee report probing Azam's shares to delay announcing its findings to the public.
Bloomberg quoted a source alleging that Anwar was worried that it may affect the outcome of the general elections, if it were to be held sometime soon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UDA names former UMW Holdings top executive as new CEO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: UDA Holdings Bhd has appointed Datuk Seri Ahmad Fuaad Kenali as chief executive officer effective from today.
With over 30 years of experience in property, automotive, utilities, media, financial services and professional consulting sectors, Fuaad has expertise in corporate strategy, business transformation, financial management and large-scale organisational turnaround.
He has also led various strategic initiatives that drive sustainable growth and organisational performance.
This includes formulating long-term direction, implementing business recovery plans, and expanding portfolios into new areas such as renewable energy and environmental services, while enhancing operational efficiency and the company's financial performance.
Previously, Fuaad held several leadership positions including UMW Holdings Bhd group chief executive officer, Malakoff Corp Bhd chief executive officer, Proton Holdings Bhd chief executive officer as well as Astro Malaysia Holdings Bhd chief financial officer.
He was also a former partner at Ernst & Young and held various senior leadership positions at DRB-HICOM Bhd and Petaling Garden Bhd.
Fuaad said: "I am deeply honoured by the trust placed in me to lead UDA at this important juncture, and I am committed to strengthening the business fundamentals and accelerating the implementation of transformation initiatives to ensure the company continues to grow and create sustainable value for all stakeholders.
"With the support of the board of directors and the entire UDA workforce, I am confident that we can continue to drive growth and realise the company's aspirations."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank explores collaboration with Media Prima
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) is exploring potential areas of collaboration with Media Prima Bhd, particularly in digital services, following a visit to the media group's headquarters at Balai Berita in Bangsar here.
Maybank president and group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Khairussaleh Ramli said the visit provided valuable insights into Media Prima's offerings and opened up opportunities for collaboration between the two organisations.
"We now have a better understanding of the services offered by Media Prima and its subsidiaries, and we are keen to explore areas of collaboration.
"One area that stood out is digital. We are focused on enhancing our digital services, and Media Prima also has strong digital capabilities that we can explore together," he said.
Khairussaleh, accompanied by group chief financial officer Shafiq Abdul Jabbar, was received by Media Prima group managing director Datuk Mohd Rafiq Mat Razali.
Also present were group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin; Media Prima news and current affairs division group managing editor Jasbant Singh; and deputy group managing editor of news and current affairs and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim.
The visit began with a tour of Media Prima's broadcast and digital facilities, including Media Prima Audio, the News and Current Affairs studios, Rev Media Group and the editorial floor.
It concluded with a corporate briefing on Media Prima's business operations and strategic outreach, followed by a discussion.
Maybank is the largest bank in Malaysia and the main banker for Media Prima.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perodua backs clarity on NAP as ministry defends EV policy stance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua Sdn Bhd (Perodua) has backed Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry's clarification on the National Automotive Policy (NAP), underscoring its role in attracting investments and strengthening Malaysia's economic base.
The national carmaker described the current guidelines as a crucial step towards building a more resilient automotive ecosystem, delivering broad-based gains across the industry.
These include sustained job creation, infrastructure investments, human capital development, research and development (R&D), technology transfer and stronger support for local entrepreneurs spanning suppliers and distributors.
"The Next Generation Vehicle (NxGV) vision under the NAP is expected to deliver better mobility value and innovation for the public, alongside broader industry services, improved safety and technology-driven mobile integration.
"The policy, vision and roadmap set out by Miti provide a comprehensive, transparent and consistent guide for automotive industry players," Perodua said in a statement.
It commended the ministry's push to position Malaysia's automotive sector as progressive, balancing growth with openness to ensure shared benefits.
The remarks come as the ministry moved to allay concerns over BYD potentially reassessing plans to set up a local assembly plant for full CKD operations in Tanjung Malim, Perak.
Its minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani stressed that Malaysia's EV policy is applied uniformly across all industry players, noting that the conditions imposed on BYD are not unique to the Chinese automaker.
He added that Malaysia remains open to Chinese automotive investments, with 14 of the 34 foreign carmakers currently operating in the country originating from China.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman lured by luxury admits role in RM3.4mil theft
Article:
BENTONG: Driven by the lure of luxury and wealth, a young woman and her husband carried out a daring RM3.4 million heist targeting a prominent entrepreneur.
"Driven by an overwhelming desire for luxury, I took part in a RM3.4 million heist involving cash and jewellery belonging to a well-known female entrepreneur," said 24-year-old convict Ayu (not her real name).
She said the crime, orchestrated by her brother-in-law, sister and husband, took place two years ago. The audacious operation succeeded and drew widespread media coverage.
She said that as a villager without higher education, the allure of a luxurious lifestyle – owning branded clothing and vehicles – led her to act without thinking, despite knowing the consequences if caught.
"For two months, we conducted surveillance around the entrepreneur's residence in Gombak, Selangor. She frequently flaunted her wealth on social media, and that made her a target.
"The four of us took about an hour and a half to search the residence, which had no security guards. We then stole a safe containing RM800,000 in cash and jewellery."
"The 180kg safe was carried away, and it took us about four hours to open it using various tools. The effort eventually paid off," she said.
Ayu and her husband are now serving time in Bentong Prison.
She said the mastermind was her brother-in-law, who had extensive experience breaking into homes and stealing cash and valuables from wealthy individuals.
Ayu said she spent RM1.4 million of the illicit gains on three luxury vehicles, paid for in cash. The money was also used to buy various items, including expensive branded clothing.
"We were arrested about a month after the crime, after police tracked our movements based on the closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage taken from outside the entrepreneur's home."
"The court sentenced me to four years and eight months' imprisonment for two offences to run concurrently. Another offence involved stealing RM800,000 in cash from a durian crepe entrepreneur's home in Penang," she said.
She added that in Bentong Prison, she spent her time in the pastry workshop preparing cookies and festive treats with about 40 other inmates.
She said she had begun to feel remorse, something she never expected as a convict.
"My husband and I will be released next July, while my sister and brother-in-law are currently in Seberang Perai Prison, Penang. This (the sentence) has made me reflect... I plan to rebuild my life.
"My advice to young people is not to be easily swayed by the desire for wealth, which can lead to crime. It will never end well," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM128.66mil Monet sale boosts 1MDB asset recovery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The sale of a high-value painting by French master Claude Monet for 25.22 million euros (about RM128.66 million) has strengthened Malaysia's ongoing efforts to recover assets linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Special Operations Division senior director Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin confirmed the transaction, saying authorities have identified both the artwork and the proceeds, which are to be returned to the government.
"It is a Monet work, and the proceeds from the sale are being held by a prominent French law firm, Archipel," he said.
Sources said the painting is believed to be part of Monet's renowned Water Lilies series, titled Nymphéas avec Reflets de Hautes Herbes, although official confirmation of the exact piece is still pending.
The artwork was previously seized by the United States Department of Justice (DOJ) in July 2016 as part of its global asset recovery initiative linked to 1MDB.
It is understood the sale was conducted under DOJ instructions and managed during court proceedings in Switzerland, with the piece ultimately acquired by a private entity, making its return to Malaysia unlikely.
Monet, a central figure of the Impressionist movement, is among the world's most sought-after artists, with works from his Water Lilies series often fetching between US$100 million and US$150 million at auction.
The Monet sale forms part of a broader recovery exercise involving at least 12 artworks linked to the 1MDB scandal.
Four paintings are currently en route back to Malaysia, while another piece believed to be by Pablo Picasso remains in Switzerland.
Six additional artworks previously owned by 1MDB-linked witness Jasmine Loo are still under the custody of Christie's and can only be released through a United States court order.
Among the pieces expected to arrive in Malaysia within two weeks are works by Picasso, Balthus, Maurice Utrillo and
Joan Miró
, as authorities continue efforts to reclaim assets tied to the scandal.
Yesterday, it was reported that four paintings valued at nearly RM1 million, including a work by Pablo Picasso, are set to be repatriated to Malaysia in connection with the 1MDB fund misappropriation scandal.
The artworks form part of a wider collection of 12 high-value paintings linked to the case, with a combined estimated value exceeding RM142 million (US$30 million).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang says no to WFH for civil servants
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has decided not to adopt the proposed work-from-home (WFH) policy for its civil servants.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the WFH policy was not suitable for all types of work, and there was no urgent need for it at this time.
"The state executive council discussed and decided on the matter.
"We have no plans to implement the WFH policy," he told newsmen in his office at Komtar here today.
Last week, Chow said the state government was weighing the possibility of adopting a WFH directive for civil servants following rising fuel costs linked to global conflict.
He said the
state
would first study the federal government's directive before deciding how best to implement it locally.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reportedly urged the private sector to adopt flexible work arrangements, following the government's efforts to expand such practices within the civil service.
The move comes as the government seeks to mitigate the impact of rising costs caused by global supply disruptions, particularly in fuel and essential goods, amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.
Anwar said initiatives such as WFH arrangements would be rolled out in stages for civil servants and could assist Malaysians in coping with economic pressures arising from global developments.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New stricter RON95 and diesel rules take effect nationwide today
Article:
KUCHING: Three new policies regulating the sale of RON95 petrol and diesel come into force today, marking the start of a stricter enforcement phase nationwide to curb subsidy leakages as well as fuel smuggling.
Among the regulations implemented are a ban on the purchase of RON95 by foreign vehicles and
restrictions on the use of international credit and debit cards
at self-service pump terminals, ensuring that subsidised fuel is only utilised by Malaysians.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the measures were taken following an increase in demand and the growing risk of leakages, especially in border areas.
He explained that the implementation of the new policies is supported by Op TIRIS 4.0, which is carried out on a larger scale through intensive patrols, deployment of static officers, undercover intelligence operations, and closed circuit television (CCTV) monitoring at high-risk stations.
"We are obtaining cooperation from oil companies. They will provide information if there are stations that suddenly record a sharp increase in sales.
"From there, our officers will go in and be deployed statically; they will wait and observe whether the sales are genuinely legitimate or if something suspicious is taking place," he said at a press conference after inspecting the implementation of the ban on RON95 purchases by foreigners and the diesel filling limit, which begins today, in Bau.
He said reports of any abuse or suspicious activity should be made directly to the ministry's operations room, which operates seven days a week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court rejects Semantan Estate's bid for pre-Merdeka land records
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed all discovery applications filed by Semantan Estate (1952) Sdn Bhd seeking pre-Merdeka land records for its compensation claim over the "Duta Enclave" land.
Judge Roslan Mat Nor ruled against the company's requests for survey plans, acquisition records, Memoranda of Transfer, and zoning documents dating back to the 1950s.
The applications were filed ahead of compensation assessment proceedings, following an appellate court ruling that the government had trespassed on the 263.27-acre site, but that any compensation should be calculated based on its value as of Dec 3, 1956.
The case arises from a long-running dispute in which the Court of Appeal last year held that Semantan could not reclaim the land, buildings, or title, despite a 2009 decision confirming government trespass and a payment of just RM1.32 million in 1956.
Instead, the appellate court ordered that compensation be assessed by the High Court according to the land's 1956 market value, with appropriate adjustments, including interest.
The Federal Court later dismissed Semantan's application for leave to appeal, effectively upholding the appellate court's ruling.
The contested site now accommodates several major government facilities, including the Inland Revenue Board headquarters, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Academy, the National Archives, and the Kuala Lumpur Syariah Court complex.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RON95 still being sold to foreign vehicles in Rantau Panjang despite ban
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: Several petrol stations here were found to still be selling RON95 to foreign-registered vehicles despite the enforcement of a nationwide ban beginning today.
Checks at a number of stations near the border revealed that some vehicles, particularly motorcycles bearing Thai registration plates, were continuing to refuel with the subsidised petrol.
When approached, a station worker claimed they did not notice the foreign-registered motorcycles due to the high volume of customers.
Observations also uncovered a different modus operandi, with foreign motorcyclists parking about 100m away from stations before walking in with small containers concealed in black plastic bags.
They were seen filling the containers with RON95 before returning to their motorcycles and leaving the area.
In some cases, the same individuals were observed repeating the act within an hour.
Local resident Azhar Abdullah, 48, said the situation at several stations in Rantau Panjang appeared quieter this morning, with most foreign vehicles opting to refuel with RON97 instead.
"Some stations are strict and do not allow foreign vehicles to purchase RON95. However, it is still common to see individuals flouting the rules, especially when enforcement officers are not present.
"There are also vehicles believed to be used for smuggling, such as Proton Waja and Mitsubishi Pajero, which take an unusually long time to refuel and appear to dominate certain stations.
"Nevertheless, I have noticed a positive change as such activities seem to be decreasing. I hope authorities will continue enforcement to curb fuel leakages," he said.
It was previously reported that, effective April 1, foreign-registered vehicles are prohibited from purchasing RON95 in Malaysia.
The enforcement is being carried out under Op Tiris 4.0 to combat the misuse and smuggling of subsidised fuel, particularly in border areas.
The public has been urged to report suspicious activities to the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Macpma supports Miti's stance on aligning automotive investments with national objectives
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Automotive Component Parts Manufacturers (Macpma) supports the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry's (Miti) position that automotive investment incentives and manufacturing licences should be aligned with national development objectives.
It said the incentives include technology transfer, capability upgrading, and the continued growth of local Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 component manufacturers.
"Such an approach is critical to sustaining and strengthening the established vendor ecosystem that has been built through decades of investment and industrial development," it said in a statement today.
Macpma also expressed its full support for Miti's clarification on Malaysia's automotive investment policies, which are applied uniformly and consistently to all investors.
"We acknowledge Miti's commitment to maintaining an open and welcoming investment environment while ensuring that new automotive investments contribute meaningfully to the long-term sustainability of the national automotive ecosystem.
"The emphasis on export orientation, localisation, and integration with the domestic supply chain is essential to strengthening Malaysia's industrial base and trade competitiveness," it said.
Furthermore, the association concurs with the ministry that these policies are developmental in nature rather than protectionist, ensuring that foreign investments complement and strengthen local industry players while creating sustainable value across the automotive value chain.
"As the representative body for Malaysia's automotive component manufacturers, we remain committed to working closely with MITI and other stakeholders to support policies that promote inclusive growth, resilience, and the long-term competitiveness of the Malaysian automotive ecosystem," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian, 38, jailed for failing to pay fine for assaulting partner, 56
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An Indonesian man was sentenced to 14 months in jail after failing to pay a RM15,000 fine imposed by the magistrate's court here today for assaulting his partner and overstaying in the country.
Accused Sudirman, 38, pleaded guilty to both charges before magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
In the first charge under Section 323 of the Penal Code, the father of three was accused of voluntarily causing hurt to his 56-year-old partner by repeatedly punching her in the face, causing injuries.
The offence was committed at a house in Sri Medan, near here, about 11.30pm on March 22.
In the second charge under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, punishable under Section 15(4) of the same act, the accused was found to have overstayed in the country without reasonable cause.
The offence was committed at the compound of the Sri Medan police station.
According to the facts of the case, the victim lodged a report stating that the accused had assaulted her during an altercation at a house.
Investigations revealed that prior to the incident, the accused and the victim had argued over financial matters.
The accused lost his temper and repeatedly punched the victim in the face.
Acting on information, police arrested the accused about 12pm on March 23.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Tan Fong Ling.
For the first charge, the court imposed a fine of RM2,000, in default six months' jail, and for the second charge, a fine of RM13,000, in default eight months' jail.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fatal crash on Penang Bridge sparks 7km traffic jam
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A fatal accident on the Penang Bridge caused severe traffic congestion stretching 7km during the morning peak today, leaving commuters stranded for hours.
Early reports indicated the crash, believed to involve a car and a motorcycle, occurred on the bridge's mainland-bound carriageway, blocking at least two lanes. The motorcyclist was pronounced dead at the scene.
Authorities and emergency responders were swiftly deployed to manage the situation. According to Plus Trafik, the incident took place at Km2.4 of the bridge.
"Latest status: right and middle lanes remain closed for police investigation and diversion work. Traffic is congested over 7km. Plan your journey," the traffic management agency posted on its X account at 8.31am.
The obstruction caused significant disruption along one of Malaysia's busiest cross-strait links, with motorists reporting standstill conditions and congestion extending several kilometres from both ends of the bridge.
Traffic monitoring sources said delays worsened as additional vehicles continued to enter the already jammed stretch.
The 13.5km Penang Bridge, which connects the island to the mainland, serves as a vital artery for daily commuters and commercial traffic, making it highly susceptible to major disruptions in the event of accidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy Commission chief: Brace for rises in energy costs
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia must be prepared to face rising fuel costs for electricity generation following the current geopolitical situation in the Middle East.
Energy Commission chief executive officer Siti Safinah Salleh said about 80 per cent of the country's natural gas cost for power generation is still fixed or tied to domestic gas.
She said the remaining 20 per cent is imported, exposing the country to global market prices.
As such, she said that natural gas costs are partially shielded from the global energy market, which has been affected by the ongoing crisis.
"However, there is still some impact as it is linked to the Market Reference Price (MRP).
"There will still be an effect on prices, but there is a ceiling cap. This cap is lower than the exposure based on global market prices," she said at a press conference during the Energy Commission's Annual Regulatory Review here.
Siti Safinah said most of the fuel supply is sourced from Australia, not the Middle East, under contracts that have already been finalised.
However, she added that standard contracts are still indexed to oil market prices.
In 2025, 33.5 per cent of Malaysia's electricity generation was produced using natural gas.
Siti Safinah said the commission is monitoring rising coal prices, which account for 58.5 per cent of the country's power generation.
She said, during previous crises, particularly the Russia-Ukraine war in 2022, global demand shifted towards coal, driving up prices.
"The commission has begun to observe an increase in coal prices. There is no sharp spike yet, but prices are trending upwards.
"As we know, Malaysia imports 100 per cent of its coal for power generation.
"Although there are some protective measures in place, we still need to be prepared for higher fuel costs."
Malaysia sources about 65 per cent of its coal supply from Indonesia and around 20 per cent from Australia, with the remainder from other countries.
The commission expects fuel cost adjustments to take place in the near future once these costs enter the system.
Siti Safinah said the increase in fuel costs is expected to be reflected through the Automatic Fuel Adjustment (AFA) mechanism.
"If costs rise, consumers will have to bear the additional charges. Adjustments through the AFA are made monthly as long as cost changes remain within a 10 per cent threshold.
"If the increase exceeds 10 per cent, we will seek government approval, and implementation will depend on the government's decision.
"Costs are still costs and must be paid, but how they are passed on depends on the government."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SUPP president: Lily Yong’s sacking under legal process
Article:
KUCHING, April 1 — The sacking of Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) Kuching chairman Datuk Lily Yong Lee Lee is currently under a “legal process”, said Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian.
According to the SUPP president, this prevented him from commenting on the matter.
“The secretary-general will issue a statement because it is a legal process so I cannot comment,” he told reporters after officiating at the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) Sarawak’s Integrity Day 2026 here.
SUPP’s leadership has yet to address Yong’s expulsion last month along with branch secretary Voong Nam Jing.
On March 26, SUPP Kuching Youth chief Nicholas Wung confirmed that the party’s Central Working Committee (CWC) had sacked the two leaders.
He told a press conference that the CWC had issued a letter dated March 16 stating that Yong and Voong were expelled from the party effective March 9.
According to the notice, they have 30 days from the date of the letter to appeal to the CWC, with the deadline falling on April 15. — The Borneo Post

Title: Fahmi says stricter penalties for fatal drunk driving not yet discussed, prosecutions ongoing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that decisions to prosecute individuals suspected of breaking the law, including cases involving drunk drivers, fall under the authority of the Attorney General, with investigations guided by existing legal frameworks and evidence.
He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) conduct inquiries not only under road transport laws but also on broader legal grounds when sufficient evidence exists to justify prosecution.
“The Cabinet did not specifically discuss this matter today, as prosecution proceedings were ongoing during the meeting. It may be reviewed next week.
“Since the case is active, we respect the process. For now, the actions are based on existing law concerning murder charges.
"The matter will proceed according to the Attorney General’s office regarding legal aspects,” he said during the ministry's weekly press conference here today.
Earlier today, a 28-year-old man was formally charged in Klang Magistrate’s Court with the murder of a motorcyclist following a fatal collision last Sunday.
He was found to be under the influence of both alcohol and drugs at the time of the crash.
Saktygaanapathy Ravichandran faces a charge under Section 302 of the Penal Code for the death of 33-year-old motorcyclist Amirul Hafiz Omar.
If convicted, the offence carries the mandatory death penalty, or alternatively, a prison term of between 30 and 40 years, along with a minimum of 12 strokes of the cane.

Title: MOE denies viral statement on SPM 2025 achievement by race, says deputy minister
Article:
DENGKIL, April 1 — A viral press statement displaying the percentage of student achievement by race for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results is untrue, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh.
He said that the Ministry of Education (MOE) did not issue the statement, and the information was not part of the official results announcement made yesterday.
“What has gone viral is 101 per cent incorrect. That is definitely not true,” he told reporters after a working visit to the Replacement Building Project (Daif 2022) at Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Dengkil here today.
Wong said the conduct of the SPM examination and the announcement of results were carried out in accordance with established benchmarks and standards, without any compromise.
“For this SPM, we conducted the examination in compliance with all set benchmarks and standards for every single subject. (Similarly) for the date of the results announcement... we ensured the results were released at 10 am simultaneously nationwide,” he said.
He added that announcing the SPM results nationwide in a uniform manner ensures transparency.
“This is a standard that the MOE will not compromise on,” he assured.
Wong was responding to a viral press statement with the MOE logo circulating on social media since yesterday, which contains student achievement percentages by race.
The statement included an analysis claiming that Malay candidates who took the 2025 SPM had the lowest pass percentage compared to Chinese and Indian students. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar to meet state leaders Thursday over global energy crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is set to meet all Chief Ministers and Menteris Besar tomorrow to discuss the ongoing global energy crisis and its implications for Malaysia’s economy.
He said several key issues will be examined during the meeting, with a briefing to follow outlining the decisions taken.
“God willing, there will be several press conferences in the afternoon led by Deputy Prime Minister II Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, and potentially statements from the prime minister himself following the meeting,” he said at the ministry’s weekly press conference today.
In a Facebook post yesterday, Anwar urged Malaysians to brace for a more difficult global energy environment as tensions in the Middle East continue to rise.
The prime minister, who also serves as Finance Minister I, said the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) had held in-depth discussions on the deteriorating global energy situation and its possible impact on the country’s economy and the welfare of the rakyat.
“The global landscape is becoming increasingly uncertain and challenging, and Malaysia will inevitably feel the effects.
“We must not be complacent, but instead remain disciplined, resilient and united in facing these pressures,” he said.
He added that despite prevailing uncertainties, Malaysia’s economic fundamentals remain solid and resilient.
He also urged Malaysians to bolster the domestic economy by prioritising local products and supporting homegrown businesses, stressing that strong internal capacity is a crucial buffer against external shocks.

Title: SJKT Dengkil redevelopment ahead of schedule, completion expected early, says deputy education minister
Article:
DENGKIL, April 1 — The redevelopment project for dilapidated buildings at Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Dengkil here is progressing smoothly and is ahead of schedule, with completion expected earlier than originally planned, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh.
Wong said the project’s physical progress has reached 65.14 per cent compared to the original target of about 50 per cent in March, placing it 15.14 per cent ahead of schedule and approximately 70 days ahead of the contract timeline.
“With this excellent contract performance, we hope to bring forward the project completion date from the original target of Oct 12 this year.
“Based on current progress, we are working to accelerate it further to about 90 days ahead, but what is important is that the project is expected to be completed before the contract period ends,” he told reporters after a working visit to the project site today.
Elaborating, Wong said the RM13.3 million project was approved in 2022 under the Second Rolling Plan of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP) to fully replace the old buildings, which had been classified as dilapidated and unsafe.
He said the project scope includes the construction of two main blocks — an administration block and an academic block housing eight classrooms — as well as a new preschool building with two classrooms and a conducive open hall space.
The new block that houses pre-school students is almost complete as part of the Replacement Building Project (Daif 2022) at the Dengkil Tamil National Type School April 1, 2026. — Bernama pic
“Once completed, all facilities including desks, chairs and learning amenities will be fully in place, enabling teaching and learning to be carried out immediately in a more comfortable environment,” he said.
He added that the overall project also includes the construction of a new sewerage system and basic facilities, including furniture and learning infrastructure.
“Although there are minor issues involving coordination with agencies such as Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) and Air Selangor, we are confident these can be resolved through close cooperation,” he said.
Wong said student safety remains a priority throughout the project as the school continues to operate with staff and students present during school days.
“I observed that safety aspects such as hoarding installation and site management have been very well implemented by the contractor and the Public Works Department Malaysia,” he said.
On recurring flood issues at the school, he said improvements to the internal drainage system are being carried out under the project, while awaiting the implementation of a flood mitigation project by the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA) at a nearby river. — Bernama

Title: CEO: Energy Commission reviews legal, regulatory frameworks to support sector stability, align with energy needs
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 1 — The Energy Commission (ST) is reviewing several legal and regulatory frameworks, including mechanisms for electricity cross-border trading, as part of its efforts to support energy sector stability and sustainable growth.
Its chief executive officer, Siti Safinah Salleh, said the commission is currently assessing existing policies while developing new frameworks to ensure the sector remains aligned with future requirements.
Also under review are the incentive-based regulation mechanisms for Regulatory Period 5 (RP5 2028—2030), which will begin in 2028, and mechanisms for electricity cross-border trading for import and export.
“We are currently reviewing and also developing several legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure that the sector remains aligned with future requirements and policies,” she said at the ST Annual Regulatory Review (STARR) 2026 here today.
She said the commission is looking to enhance open grid access, or third-party access, through initiatives such as the Corporate Renewable Energy Supply Scheme (CRESS) and the Community Renewable Energy Aggregation Mechanism (CREAM).
“(The review also covers) the public electricity distribution within dedicated areas, electricity supply Acts, as well as revisions, particularly to enforcement provisions, and technical standards for quality and safety.
“These efforts are aimed at ensuring that the regulatory framework remains relevant, robust and capable of supporting a more complex and dynamic energy landscape,” she said.
Meanwhile, ST, in a statement, said the commission maintains a strong focus on practical regulatory implementation, strengthening frameworks, standards, and market arrangements to support sector stability and sustainable growth.
This includes the enforcement of the Energy Efficiency and Conservation Act (EECA) 2024, improvements in tariff mechanisms, and continued expansion of regulatory frameworks to support higher renewable energy (RE) development.
The review is anchored on ST’s six key regulatory pillars, covering: safety and enforcement; system reliability and energy security; fair and transparent tariffs; energy efficiency and sustainability; energy transition and market development; and governance, stakeholder engagement and institutional strengthening.
“Across these pillars, the energy sector recorded tangible progress. Notably, improvements were seen in safety outcomes, with lower electrical and gas-related incidents, while regulatory enhancements and licensing oversight continued to strengthen compliance across the sector,” the statement said. — Bernama

Title: Petronas operations stable, weekly fuel prices to be announced every Wednesday, says comms minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 1 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that Petronas operations remain stable for the period between April and May, following a detailed report presented at yesterday’s National Economic Action Council (NEAC) meeting, where several aspects of the company’s operations were carefully reviewed.
He also said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed that the situation continue to be closely monitored.
“For the period between April and May, Petronas operations remain under control.
"At the same time, we are also informed that several vessels are still in transit and awaiting passage through the Strait of Hormuz,” he said during the ministry's weekly press conference here today.
He then dismissed claims that Malaysia had paid any tolls, attributing such reports to misunderstandings or misinformation circulating online.
The minister also confirmed that weekly fuel prices for RON95, RON97, and diesel will be announced every Wednesday.
“Any reports or information received outside these official announcements after 6pm should be regarded as false.
“Domestic fuel supply is currently under control, and the matter will be further reviewed at next week’s NEAC meeting, with an official statement expected thereafter,” he added.

Title: Johor aims to lead in FDI, strengthens Johor‑Singapore SEZ framework, says Hasni
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, April 1 — Johor is committed to sustaining its position as a leading investment destination and to remain at the forefront of foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows, which reached RM110.1 billion in 2025, the highest among all states.
Johor State Economic and Investment Advisor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad said the state will not remain complacent despite its strong performance, but will enhance its competitiveness and investment ecosystem continuously.
He also said the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) will serve as a key blueprint to provide clarity and certainty for both new and existing investors, while also enabling the state to retain and expand current investments.
“What is important now is for Johor to maintain its momentum and remain a preferred destination for investors.
“We are not only talking about attracting investments, but also reclaiming and strengthening investments that are already in Johor. The JS-SEZ will address uncertainties and provide a clear framework for investors,” he said during the Asia Summit on Advanced Innovation & Manufacturing 2026 here today.
Efforts to enhance Johor-Singapore cross-border connectivity are also being accelerated under the JS-SEZ, including the QR code immigration clearance, digital cargo systems, and the upcoming Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link.
Hasni said the RTS Link, scheduled to commence in January 2027, is expected to carry up to 10,000 passengers per hour each direction, potentially transforming travel patterns across one of the world’s busiest land border crossings. It currently records about 300,000 daily travellers, including 100,000 motorcyclists.
“We cannot afford any glitches in our systems, whether it is the QR code clearance, digital cargo, or the RTS itself. These initiatives must work seamlessly to support growing cross-border movement,” he said.
Hasni, who is also Johor Economic, Tourism and Cultural Office Singapore (JETCO) executive chairman,  said the improved connectivity could significantly alter commuting trends, with more efficient travel potentially increasing movement between Johor and Singapore in both directions.
On the industrial front, he said Johor currently hosts the largest number of data centres in Southeast Asia, and accounts for more than 50 per cent of the state’s total FDI last year.
He acknowledged, however, that the sector’s high demand for electricity and water presents sustainability challenges, particularly amid climate change and resource constraints.
“We must move away from reliance on coal-based energy and transition towards renewable energy to remain attractive to investors, while ensuring long-term sustainability,” he said.
The state has established the Johor Sustainability Centre to drive green initiatives and technological adoption to support this transition, while encouraging industries to improve energy efficiency, he said.
A total of 126 companies have been identified as managing energy inefficiently, and the government is offering incentives and tax allowances to encourage a shift towards renewable energy usage, he said. — Bernama

Title: Police credit Volunteer Smartphone Patrol app for crime drop during Hari Raya
Article:
